about.subheading: The lab's anti-fishing discipline: pre-committed specs, structural workflow, and epistemic honesty.

intro.p1: The lab designs and deploys hedging instruments validated by structural econometrics. Each iteration follows a strict five-step protocol to ensure results reflect the evidence, not the analyst's design choices.
intro.p2: Rejected iterations carry equal narrative weight as approved ones. The iteration record is public and immutable.

step_01.title: Spec
step_01.body: The decision-citation block is drafted and recorded before any code runs. It defines Y, M, X, the model, null hypotheses, rejection thresholds, and sample size criteria.

step_02.title: Data
step_02.body: Data is downloaded from the primary source with a verification hash. No data file is manually edited. The verification trio (source, date, hash) is recorded in the notebook.

step_03.title: Estimation
step_03.body: The pre-committed estimator is run. β, standard errors, CI, and fit statistics are recorded without post-hoc specification selection.

step_04.title: Tests
step_04.body: Pre-committed sensitivity tests are applied: alternative specifications, sample subsets, heteroskedasticity tests, and extreme-value analysis. Each code cell is followed by a verification trio checkpoint.

step_05.title: Disposition
step_05.body: The disposition memo is written: PASS if the result clears pre-committed thresholds; FAIL otherwise. FAIL is not archived or minimized. It is published with the same weight as PASS.

commitments.item_2: Trio checkpoint after every code cell
commitments.item_4: Rejection carries equal narrative weight as approval
